entries.title: Beyond lithium: how sodium-ion batteries could change the world
entries.summary: Nature, Published online: 15 July 2026; doi:10.1038/d41586-026-02150-yBatteries based on sodium ions are entering mass production. Some researchers say they could ultimately be a cheaper, safer alternative to lithium in electric cars and other energy applications.

entries.title: How the calendar came to be
entries.summary: Nature, Published online: 14 July 2026; doi:10.1038/d41586-026-02102-6The development of time-keeping, and a book that brings an immune cell back in vogue, in this week’s pick from the Nature archive.

entries.title: ‘Dark comet’ unmasked by its mysterious motions
entries.summary: Nature, Published online: 14 July 2026; doi:10.1038/d41586-026-02192-2A celestial body thought to be an ordinary space rock turns out to have a comet-like tail.

entries.title: Seeking an industry role after your PhD? Make sure your CV reflects that
entries.summary: Nature, Published online: 14 July 2026; doi:10.1038/d41586-026-01855-4A doctorate can open many doors. A CV tailored to your planned career path can help to unlock them.

entries.title: Africa’s response to this Ebola outbreak shows how to shape global health
entries.summary: Nature, Published online: 14 July 2026; doi:10.1038/d41586-026-02148-6Countries elsewhere must learn from the continent’s example and combat disease outbreaks by working together, not by closing borders.

entries.title: DNA-shredding CRISPR enzyme takes aim at cancer cells
entries.summary: Nature, Published online: 14 July 2026; doi:10.1038/d41586-026-02122-2A bacterial self-destruct mechanism has been repurposed as a potential therapy, selectively eliminating diseased cells — including tumour cells in mouse models of cancer.
